summary: This notebook serves as a beginner's guide to the FIDE & Google Efficient Chess AI Challenge, demonstrating how to set up the Kaggle chess environment, write a rule-based agent, and submit it for evaluation. It walks through installing dependencies, parsing FEN board states using the Chessnut library, and generating legal moves. The agent implements a simple priority heuristic that checks for checkmates, captures, and queen promotions before falling back to random moves. Readers learn the complete workflow for creating, testing, and packaging a chess agent for this specific competition.
competition_flow: Sets up the Kaggle chess environment, writes a rule-based agent that parses FEN states and applies a priority heuristic for moves, tests it against a random opponent, and explains the submission format.
insights: The Kaggle chess environment can be programmatically controlled using the kaggle-environments package and the Chessnut library for board state parsing.
A simple priority-based heuristic (checkmate > capture > promotion > random) can serve as a functional baseline agent without any machine learning.
Agents must output moves in UCI notation and correctly handle the FEN string format provided by the environment observation.
